methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence

private.name: Vatn Systems

data.narrative:
Rune Technologies is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by David Tuttle and Peter Goldsborough, having raised $20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public firms operating in the defense and security sector, including peers such as Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries at similar stages.

Two valuation methodologies were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $20M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yields $40M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range between $150M and $500M. The divergence between methods reflects material heterogeneity in scale and investor appetite within the peer set.

Triangulation across both approaches supports a valuation range of $34M to $345M, with a median of $170M. The wide spread reflects significant uncertainty around which comparable set most appropriately anchors Rune's current stage and trajectory. Key variables affecting placement within this range include evidence of product differentiation, customer traction, and capital efficiency relative to peers. Stage-progression conservatism versus peer-based uplift represents the primary analytical tension.
